name: USB Serial (NMEA 0183)
description: Read any USB serial instrument that speaks NMEA 0183 — GPS, wind, depth, log, heading, water temperature, rudder — straight from a device plugged into the Raspberry Pi. Auto-detects the port and baud; every recognised value maps to its sensor slot.
author: BigaOS Team

versions.changelog: Initial release — full NMEA 0183 instrument set over USB serial, with device/baud auto-detection

dataStreams.name: GPS Position
dataStreams.description: Latitude/longitude from RMC/GGA/GLL — only when the receiver reports a valid fix

dataStreams.name: Heading (Magnetic)
dataStreams.description: Magnetic heading from HDG/HDM/VHW (auto-corrected to true using GPS declination)

dataStreams.name: Speed Through Water
dataStreams.description: Boat speed through water from VHW (speed log)

configSchema.label: About
configSchema.default: Plug a USB instrument that outputs NMEA 0183 (GPS, wind, depth, log, heading...) into the Raspberry Pi. Leave device and baud on Auto to have BigaOS find it. Recognised values appear under Settings → Data Sources and map automatically.

configSchema.label: Serial Device
configSchema.description: Serial port of the instrument, e.g. /dev/ttyUSB0 or /dev/ttyACM0. Leave as 'auto' to detect it automatically.

configSchema.label: Baud Rate
configSchema.description: Serial speed. 'Auto' tries the common rates until it finds valid data. GPS mice usually use 4800 or 9600; NMEA 0183 instruments are typically 4800.

configSchema.label: Auto Reconnect
configSchema.description: Automatically reconnect if the device is unplugged or the connection drops

configSchema.label: Reconnect Interval (seconds)
configSchema.description: Seconds to wait between reconnection attempts
